summary: Re-entry cycle 2: Fixed 2 critical routing gaps (RC-001, RC-002). Added blocked handlers for craft-tracker and continuity-tracker in orchestrator routing table. Full audit confirmed all 12 orchestrator children, 8 loop configs (auditor passed/failed/blocked), and all 20 coordinator routing tables have complete result-code coverage. Total routes: 26 orchestrator + 134 coordinator = 160. No unhandled result codes remain.

gapsAddressed.description: Orchestrator had no handler for craft-tracker result 'blocked'
gapsAddressed.fix: Added route: read=agents/craft-tracker/status.json, condition='result == blocked', action='write own status: failed'

gapsAddressed.description: Orchestrator had no handler for continuity-tracker result 'blocked'
gapsAddressed.fix: Added route: read=agents/continuity-tracker/status.json, condition='result == blocked', action='write own status: failed'

gapsAddressed.description: Audited all routing tables for blocked-handler completeness per INV-030 (No Silent Failures)
gapsAddressed.fix: Confirmed all 65 agents that define 'blocked' have corresponding handler routes in their parent's routing table. No additional changes needed.

gapsNotRequiringRoutingChanges.note: craft-profile.json single-writer change is an architecture/roster concern. Routing already dispatches craft-profile-selector only from concept-coordinator — no routing change needed.
T25 thematic argument scaffolding enforcement is a prompt-level concern for auditor agents, not a routing concern. No routing change needed.
